title: Allow CI user to cleanup own files
body:
According to https://go.dev/ref/mod#module-cache golang will leave
behind read-only bits.  It was observed that these cause the find/rm
cleanup operations to fail fail with `permission denied` on thousands
of files.  This is preventing cleanup of cruft from unrelated Cirrus-tasks
leading to unnecessary occupation of critical, local-ssd storage space.
Fix this by ensuring the user has at least write access to the entire
contents of `$TMPDIR` and `$HOME`, `ci` subdirs.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?


comments.body:
For reference, I'm seeing countless messages like the following, under the 'prep' and 'cleanup' task scripts:

```
rm: cannot remove '/Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-5483845596741632/.go/pkg/mod/github.com/containers/storage@v1.52.1-0.20240202181245-1419a5980565/pkg/system/meminfo_unsupported.go': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove '/Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-5483845596741632/.go/pkg/mod/github.com/containers/storage@v1.52.1-0.20240202181245-1419a5980565/pkg/system/path_windows_test.go': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove '/Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-5483845596741632/.go/pkg/mod/github.com/containers/storage@v1.52.1-0.20240202181245-1419a5980565/pkg/system/rm_test.go': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove '/Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-5483845596741632/.go/pkg/mod/github.com/containers/storage@v1.52.1-0.20240202181245-1419a5980565/pkg/system/path_unix.go': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove '/Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-5483845596741632/.go/pkg/mod/github.com/containers/storage@v1.52.1-0.20240202181245-1419a5980565/pkg/system/lstat_unix.go': Permission denied
```

Example Ref: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6400831147212800
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

Checking one currently unused Mac instance, it has 4 old `ci/task-*` directories occupying about 10% of the local SSD.  As these instances can host a max of 30 tasks or run for 24hours, the disk could potentially become quite full.  As we all know, SSD's slow _WAY_ down as they fill up.

Hrmmm, seems it's still leaving some other things behind, [example OSX Cross prep](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5616144380329984):

```
contrib/cirrus/mac_cleanup.sh
+ killall podman vfkit gvproxy make go ginkgo
No matching processes belonging to you were found
+ chmod -R u+w '/private/tmp/ci/*' /private/tmp/ci/.Spotlight-V100 /private/tmp/ci/.fseventsd
chmod: cannot access '/private/tmp/ci/*': No such file or directory
chmod: changing permissions of '/private/tmp/ci/.Spotlight-V100': Operation not permitted
chmod: cannot read directory '/private/tmp/ci/.Spotlight-V100': Permission denied
chmod: changing permissions of '/private/tmp/ci/.fseventsd': Operation not permitted
chmod: cannot read directory '/private/tmp/ci/.fseventsd': Permission denied
+ rm -rf '/private/tmp/ci/*' /private/tmp/ci/.Spotlight-V100 /private/tmp/ci/.fseventsd
rm: cannot remove '/private/tmp/ci/.Spotlight-V100': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove '/private/tmp/ci/.fseventsd': Permission denied
```

Edit: I took a look at that instance.  It's just those two files left-behind.  They're owned by `root:admin` so there's nothing that can be done.  I'm okay with ignoring those.
I think we can ignore them, it's just two files.  The user running tasks has no `sudo` access (on purpose).

Thanks a lot Paul, this is a great thing I can reference in the script.  My only hesitation in using `go clean -modcache` is this may not catch everything.  For example, another tool or script could easily mark something read-only (on purpose or accident).

So I still feel the "large hammer" approach is best.  We really do want absolute and final cleanup of non-current-task related stuffs.  Even if just to remove any doubt whatsoever during debugging, that it could be having any effect on current results.

force-push: Comments and commit-message update based on Paul's feedback.

Thanks again Paul.
